what_it_does: Implements the recollection-side capture source for the Company noticeboard: it reads board items through the stable `company board` CLI, converts each item into a recall-queryable recollection unit and exchange row, embeds the text via the served :8007 embedding lens, and writes both records idempotently into the recollection database under a deterministic md5 of the board:// address.
observations: Reads board data through the `company board list` and `company board get` CLI instead of company-internal files.
Registers a `board-item` unit type row via `registerUnitType`.
Computes a deterministic recollection id as md5(board://<id>) so re-captures update rather than duplicate.
Writes both an exchange row via `insertExchange` and a unit row via `insertUnit` under the same id.
Uses the per-source harness tag `board` and stores the board:// address in `sessionId`.
Probes the served :8007 embedding endpoint once before reading the board to fail fast.
Per-item parse or embed failures are counted and surfaced while the sweep continues.
Supports overriding the CLI path via `COMPANY_BOARD_CMD` for portability.
Maps a board item into the `ConversationExchange` shape so it can reuse the existing recall pipeline.
Parses CLI text output with regex heuristics keyed on markers like `type=`, `title:`, `created=`, and a trailing body block.
standouts: Fail-loud contract: refuses silent fallback for both the board CLI and the embedding lens.
Idempotent capture keyed by a deterministic md5 of the board:// address.
Maps an external board item into the internal ConversationExchange shape so it shares the same vector recall space as conversations.
Probes the embedding endpoint before any board read to fail fast rather than churning through items.
conventions: Uses INSERT OR REPLACE for idempotent writes.
Uses a per-source harness tag (`board`) aligned with the source filter.
Uses deterministic hashing for primary keys.
Reuses existing db/embed functions rather than hand-rolling writes.
Fail-loud error handling instead of silent fallbacks.
Heavy inline documentation explaining design rationale.
concerns: Parsing relies on a specific text layout from `company board get`; the parser is regex/heuristic based and could break if the CLI output format changes.
The body extraction strips only up to four leading spaces, which may corrupt indented content such as code blocks.
`BOARD_CMD.split` on whitespace does not support quoted arguments or paths containing spaces.
The pre-sweep embedding probe adds an extra round-trip before any board data is read.
`kvOnLine` assumes values end at two or more spaces, which may misparse values containing double spaces.
The `parseBoardGet` last-meta regex uses a brittle marker list and may mis-locate the body boundary.
notes: This is the ingest wire that makes Company noticeboard items searchable through the same recall pipeline as conversations, using a stable CLI boundary to avoid coupling to the company's internal file layout.
questions: What is the exact, versioned output contract of `company board list` and `company board get`?
How does the system handle a board item whose title or body contains the em-dash placeholder '—'?
What happens when `company board list` returns an item id that `company board get` then reports as missing?
Is the :8007 embedding lens expected to be running before any capture, or is there an auto-start path?
Why is the board item's `state` not included in the embedded text beyond the type prefix in userMessage?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file captures board items via the stable CLI, embeds them through the served lens, and writes idempotent recall rows, exactly as the header comment describes.
apparent_themes: Stable interface boundaries over direct file access
intention_signals: The author wants the noticeboard to be a recall source alongside conversations without hardcoding company internals, so they bridged it through a documented CLI, reused the existing exchange/unit write path, and made re-runs safe and observable.
novelty: It treats a CLI command (`company board`) as a stable, portable capture source and maps external noticeboard items into the conversation-exchange embedding schema so they share the same vector recall space.
contribution: A self-contained ingest adapter that adds a new capture source and unit type for board items, demonstrating how external company data can be mirrored into recollection without altering the core recall schema or write paths.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript module that captures Company noticeboard items via the `company board` CLI, embeds them through a served lens, and writes idempotent exchange and unit records into the recollection database keyed by md5(board://id).
intention_for_embedding: To make noticeboard items recallable alongside conversations by reusing the existing exchange embedding and unit registration pipeline.

symbols.does: Declares the TypeScript shape of a board item as returned by the CLI parser.
symbols.description: Defines the fields extracted from `company board get` output, including id, board:// address, type, state, source, title, body, author session, channel, thread, and timestamps.

symbols.does: Declares the result shape returned by a board capture sweep.

symbols.does: Runs a full board capture sweep into recollection.
symbols.description: Initializes embeddings, probes the served lens, opens the database, registers the board-item unit type, lists and iterates board items, embeds each one, writes exchange and unit records idempotently, counts failures, and closes the database if it opened it.
